Contributing to ObsidianRAG

First off, thank you for considering contributing! It’s people like you who make the open-source community such a great place to learn, inspire, and create.
1. How Can I Contribute?

    Reporting Bugs: Create an issue with a clear title and description.

    Suggesting Enhancements: Open an issue to discuss a new feature idea.

    Pull Requests:

        Fork the repo and create your branch from main.

        If you've added code that should be tested, add tests.

        Ensure the project builds/runs locally.

        Open a Pull Request!

2. Style Guide

To keep the project clean and organized, please follow these conventions:

    Code Structure: All source code lives in the src/ directory. Do not add logic files to the root directory.

    Python Style: We follow PEP 8 standards.

    Commit Messages: Use clear, imperative messages.

        Good: add: user authentication logic

        Bad: fixed some stuff

3. Setup for Development

    Clone your fork:
    Bash

    git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/YOUR_REPO.git

    Install dependencies:
    Bash

    pip install -r requirements.txt

    Create a branch for your fix:
    Bash

    git checkout -b feature/cool-new-feature

4. Code of Conduct

By participating in this project, you agree to abide by our Code of Conduct. Please be kind and respectful to all contributors.
